title: Evaluate web-tree-sitter WASM integration feasibility for CLI package
description: Evaluate and validate web-tree-sitter WASM integration in the CLI package before implementing forensic AST upgrade. Assess bundle size impact (~3.5MB WASM), Node.js runtime compatibility, WASM loading strategy (lazy vs eager), and grammar availability for JavaScript/TypeScript. Produce a brief technical decision record confirming the approach or identifying blockers. This step gates TASK-003.

files.change: Add web-tree-sitter dependency (evaluate version compatibility with existing Node.js target)
Create minimal probe that loads web-tree-sitter and parses a trivial JS file; confirm WASM loads correctly in CLI Node.js context; delete after validation or keep as test fixture

implementation: Add web-tree-sitter to packages/cli/package.json and install
Create minimal probe script that loads WASM and parses a trivial JS snippet
Test probe against Node.js version used by CLI (check engines field in package.json)
Evaluate lazy loading strategy: load WASM only when inferPatternHint() is called
Confirm tree-sitter-javascript grammar binary is available or can be loaded from npm
Document decision: bundle size impact, loading latency, and integration approach in planning-context note

reference.examples: Lazy import at call site to avoid startup overhead

criteria: web-tree-sitter installs without peer dependency conflicts
WASM loads successfully in CLI's Node.js runtime (no native module errors)
tree-sitter-javascript grammar parses a 10-line TypeScript/JS file to AST without errors
Bundle size impact documented (expected ~3.5MB WASM, acceptable for CLI package)
Decision on lazy vs eager loading documented

rationale.chosen_approach: Gated evaluation step before full implementation prevents wasted LOC if WASM integration is blocked
decision_factors: 3.5MB WASM is non-trivial for CLI startup
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds one task step but eliminates risk of discovering blockers mid-implementation

manual_checks: Run probe script and confirm AST root node is returned
Check that pnpm install succeeds with no peer dep errors
success_metrics: Probe executes in under 2 seconds in Node.js CLI context

risks.description: WASM loading fails in specific Node.js version or bundler context
risks.mitigation: Test against exact Node.js version in package.json engines field
risks.fallback: Use @ast-grep/napi or fall back to enhanced regex with structural hints

risks.description: 3.5MB WASM causes unacceptable CLI startup regression
risks.mitigation: Use lazy loading — only load WASM when scanner is explicitly invoked
risks.fallback: Ship WASM as optional peer dependency
